sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: VfB Stuttgart have been in strong form, winning 4 of their last 5 matches, including two convincing 3-0 away victories. Their attack, led by Deniz Undav (11 goals, 3 assists), has been potent, scoring late in games. In contrast, 1. FC Köln have managed only 2 wins in their last 5, struggling defensively with 3 losses and conceding in most matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Stuttgart hold a dominant record over Köln, unbeaten in their last 7 encounters with 4 wins in the last 5 head-to-heads. Their most recent meeting in September 2025 saw Stuttgart win 2-1 away, and the last match at MHP Arena ended in a 1-1 draw. Köln have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 5 visits to Stuttgart.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Stuttgart, sitting 6th with 39 points, have scored 38 goals this season (7th in Bundesliga) and boast a strong defense (4th fewest goals conceded at 28). Köln, 11th with 23 points, have scored 30 goals but conceded 34, showing defensive frailties. Stuttgart's high possession (57.8%) and corner stats (5.5 per game) highlight their control in matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Stuttgart's Deniz Undav is in excellent form with 3 goals and 3 assists in the last 5 matches, while goalkeeper Alexander Nübel has been reliable. Köln miss key midfielder Said El Mala (7 goals), which could hurt their attack. Their recent standout, Ragnar Ache, will need to step up against Stuttgart's solid defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Stuttgart's superior form, home advantage, and historical edge, they are clear favorites, aligning with Sofascore's 60% home win probability. Köln's tendency to concede late and their struggles on the road suggest a tough challenge. Expect Stuttgart to control possession and create chances, likely securing a win.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, VfB Stuttgart leads 1. FC Köln by a narrow margin of 1-0 at the MHP Arena. Stuttgart has dominated possession and created more significant opportunities, reflecting their control over the game's tempo. Köln, while showing resilience, has struggled to convert their entries into the final third into clear-cut chances. The first half saw Stuttgart capitalize on one of their big chances, with Ermedin Demirović finding the net, while Köln missed their single big opportunity. This sets up an intriguing second half as Köln looks to equalize, and Stuttgart aims to extend their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: VfB Stuttgart has been the more dominant side in terms of possession and chance creation. Their frequent entries into the penalty area and ability to win possession in the final third highlight their attacking intent. However, they have been wasteful in front of goal, missing a couple of big chances that could have put the game beyond Köln’s reach. Defensively, they’ve been solid, with players like Jeff Chabot and Maximilian Mittelstädt making key clearances. On the other hand, 1. FC Köln has shown determination with numerous final third entries and penalty area penetrations but lacks the cutting edge needed to level the score. Their aerial dominance has been notable, yet they’ve struggled to turn set-piece opportunities into goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Stuttgart, Ermedin Demirović has been pivotal, scoring the only goal of the match so far and posing a constant threat with his positioning. Deniz Undav has also been dangerous, narrowly missing a big chance, and remains a likely candidate to add to the tally. Jamie Leweling’s creativity has shone through with an assist and key passes that have unsettled Köln’s defense. For Köln, goalkeeper Marvin Schwäbe has been a standout, keeping his team in the game with crucial interventions. Jan Thielmann and Ragnar Ache have shown glimpses of danger, but their inability to convert chances has left Köln trailing.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Stuttgart started brightly with several spells of sustained pressure in the early minutes, pushing Köln onto the back foot. However, Köln responded with bursts of attacking intent, particularly in the middle of the half, where they managed to build momentum and threaten Stuttgart’s defense. As the half progressed, Stuttgart regained control with consistent waves of attack, especially around the time of their goal. Towards the end of the half, Köln mounted a late surge, showing desperation to equalize, but they couldn’t break through. This back-and-forth nature suggests both teams have the capacity for dangerous attacking spells in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Stuttgart will likely aim to capitalize on their dominance in possession and chance creation to secure the win. They may push for an early goal to kill off Köln’s hopes, leveraging players like Undav and Demirović. Köln, on the other hand, will need to be more clinical with their opportunities and may adopt a more aggressive approach to find an equalizer. Their focus on set-pieces, given their aerial strength, could be a key tactic. The game remains finely poised, and a single goal could shift the momentum decisively. Expect Stuttgart to maintain control, but Köln’s determination could lead to a tense finish if they manage to convert their chances.
